name: extract
description: Extract content from specific URLs using Tavily's extraction API. Returns clean markdown/text from web pages. Use when you have specific URLs and need their content without writing code.
capabilities: Extract clean markdown or text content from specific URLs
Support for basic and advanced extraction (handles JavaScript-rendered pages)
Query-focused extraction that reranks content by relevance
Batch extraction of up to 20 URLs per request
OAuth and API key authentication support
author: tavily-ai
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 137 potential issues across external_commands, network, filesystem, and env_access categories. After semantic evaluation, all findings are FALSE POSITIVES - these patterns represent legitimate API extraction functionality. The skill uses standard shell commands (curl, jq) to communicate with Tavily's official API, accesses environment variables for API key authentication, and reads OAuth tokens from the standard MCP auth directory. No malicious behavior, data exfiltration, or command injection vulnerabilities were identified.
valueStatement: This skill extracts clean markdown or text content from specific URLs using Tavily's extraction API. Perfect for research, documentation retrieval, and content aggregation without writing custom scraping code.
limitations: Requires Tavily API key or OAuth authentication
Some advanced features require query parameter with chunks_per_source
May not extract content from sites that block automated access

useCases.title: Research Documentation Gathering
useCases.description: Extract documentation content from multiple API reference pages to build a local knowledge base

useCases.title: Competitive Analysis
useCases.description: Extract content from competitor websites, product pages, and blog posts for market research

useCases.title: Content Aggregation
useCases.description: Pull articles and content from multiple news sources or blogs into a single markdown format

promptTemplates.title: Basic URL Extraction
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract the content from this URL: https://example.com/article
promptTemplates.scenario: When you have a single URL and want the full content

promptTemplates.title: Multiple URLs Extraction
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract content from these URLs: https://docs.example.com/api, https://docs.example.com/auth
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to gather content from multiple related pages

promptTemplates.title: Query-Focused Extraction
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract information about authentication from these URLs: https://example.com/docs, https://example.com/api-reference. Focus on API keys and OAuth.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you want relevant content filtered by a specific topic

promptTemplates.title: Advanced Extraction for Dynamic Pages
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract all content from this JavaScript-heavy page using advanced extraction: https://app.example.com/dashboard
promptTemplates.scenario: When dealing with single-page applications or JavaScript-rendered content

bestPractices: Use the query parameter to filter content to exactly what you need, especially when extracting from large pages
Start with basic extraction and only use advanced mode if content is missing or incomplete
Batch URLs by topic or category to keep results organized and relevant
antiPatterns: Extracting more than 20 URLs in a single request will fail
Using chunks_per_source without a query parameter will return an error
Not checking the failed_results field in the response may miss extraction failures

faq.question: Do I need a Tavily API key?
faq.answer: Yes, you need either a Tavily API key or an existing Tavily account for OAuth authentication. Get an API key at tavily.com or sign up for an account.

faq.question: How many URLs can I extract at once?
faq.answer: You can extract up to 20 URLs per request. For larger batches, split into multiple requests.

faq.question: What is the difference between basic and advanced extraction?
faq.answer: Basic extraction is faster and works for static HTML pages. Advanced extraction handles JavaScript-rendered pages, complex layouts, and structured data but takes longer.

faq.question: How does the query parameter work?
faq.answer: The query parameter reranks extracted content chunks by relevance to your search terms. Use it with chunks_per_source to get the most relevant sections.

faq.question: Why am I getting failed_results?
faq.answer: Failed results occur when URLs are unreachable, blocked, or timeout. Check the failed_results array in the response for specific error information.

faq.question: Can I extract content from password-protected pages?
faq.answer: No, this skill cannot extract content from pages that require login or authentication beyond what's publicly accessible.
